title: Build the SECRETS CLASSIFIER for the Apple Notes -> IKB
job:
Build the SECRETS CLASSIFIER for the Apple Notes -> IKB brain sync (the security spine of a larger sync we are designing). Create a NEW standalone repo scaffold at ~/m3ta-notes-sync/ (git init a fresh branch agent-job/secrets-classifier). Deliver ONLY:
1. secrets_classifier.py — a pure function classify(title, body) -> {"is_secret": bool, "reasons": [str]}. Detect (case-insensitive) on title AND body: sk-[a-z0-9], api[_ -]?key, access[_ -]?token, bearer, password, passphrase, "seed phrase", mnemonic, .env, -----BEGIN, "personal access token", PAT (word-boundary), "private key", client[_ -]?secret, AKIA[0-9A-Z]{6,}, "lock code", wallet, and a high-entropy base64/hex blob heuristic (>=32 chars). Bias to FALSE POSITIVES (better to over-flag). NEVER log or print the matched secret value — only the reason label.
2. test_secrets_classifier.py — pytest covering each pattern as a true positive plus clean-text true negatives (e.g. "Patricia", "Patio", "wallet of patience" should NOT trip the wallet rule via word-boundary care), and one entropy case.
3. README.md — documents that this is the mandatory FIRST stage of a one-way (Notes->vault) secrets-gated sync; the denylist + entropy approach; defense-in-depth note (allowlist of synced folders + a 99-Secrets-DO-NOT-SYNC quarantine + this scrubber).
4. .gitignore excluding *.env, *secret*, *.key, __pycache__.
CONSTRAINTS: Do NOT read, open, or copy any actual Apple Notes / NoteStore.sqlite content. Do NOT implement the sync runner or taxonomy mapper (leave as TODO-stub files). Run the tests, make them pass, commit on the branch. Report the file paths and test results.
